However: (1) Outline width is inconsistent — yellow/gold border ring varies between 1px and 2px thick in several places around the heart perimeter, most visible at top-left lobe curve; (2) Scatter/sparkle pixels around the heart are noticeably asymmetric — right side has denser clustering than left with no obvious intentional directionality; (3) Green interior has a few off-tone pixels that could indicate noise rather than deliberate dithering; (4) Some yellow pixels bleed into the green interior zone at the boundary without clear intent. Hard tones are used (green, gold, near-black) with no gradient shading — a positive sign. No melting details, no semantic failures (hearts are recognizable). The inconsistent outline thickness is the strongest AI-generation tell here, as hand-crafted pixel art would typically maintain uniform border width. Marginal overall — passes pixel art aesthetic but the outline inconsistency and asymmetry prevent a clean pass.
